entry.title: Re: Joule Studio Integration with Mapbox MCP
entry.content:
Hi  Please try to add the header authorisation this way -

using the property - 'URL.headers.Authorization'

Warm Regards,

Santaneel

@Tirthankar_Chakraborty1

entry.title: SAP BusinessObjects BI platform taking considerable time for "Running command" on updates?
entry.content:
We run multiple versions of SAP BusinessObjects - Platform Administration BI 4.3 and BI 2025 on inexpensive Azure machines, which is likely a factor. We continually spend 30+ minutes if not hours while "running command".  The web applications have been updated, Tomcat has been restarted, and it looks like we're at about 99.999997% completion. Yet here we are.

Running command near end of upgrade?

 Customers report that the operation can take 5-15 minutes on their systems. Longer than desirable, yet manageable. So far, I've waited two hours.

Unfortunately, nothing is being written to the installData logs during this step, so we have nothing actionable for making the install go faster.

Does this situation happen to anyone else?!?

entry.title: Re: Week 4 : Use Joule Predictive Code Completion to Implement Determination
entry.content:
#Challenge completed for Week - 4, Package : ZAC_1620808
Completed all the challenges. Package - ZAC_1620808

entry.title: fg order start & end date consider production start and end date of SFG based on delivery time
entry.content:
Dear Team,

In SAP Public cloud, there is requirement that production start and end date of finished material should consider the its component SFG material start and end date, based on that only finished production /planned order start and end time must be calculated. It must consider the delivery date of sales order as well as delivery time of its component material. The date must have the detailed list of header components materials level start and end date dependency in subsequent level of BOM components.

 

There is approx. 10 levels of Header BOM. how the system can meet this requirement in SAP Public cloud.

 

Please suggest how can i meet this requirement.

entry.title: Re: Week 3 : Use RAP Business Logic Prediction to Implement Validation
Looking for repeatable step/command to confirm connectivity to HSM within ASE
entry.content:
Once Sybase ASE instance configured for using an external key (HSM via PKCS) is restarted there is generally minimal interaction with the HSM (Appears once decryption of the keys take place then)

I would like to see if there are any steps, or a specific command that would confirm connectivity to the HSM (to ensure availability). Could you please advise?

In summary I would like to ifunderstand  there were any commands or functions that would safely confirm access to the HSM (ie a command that completes successfully, reports upon availability - or is guaranteed to cause using the HSM) without any destructive action.


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

SAP ASE configured to use an external keysore (HSM) via PKCS library

sp_configure 'external keystore'
go

sp_encryption 'hsm_credential',  'lib=libPortalPKCS11.so; pin=xxxxxxxx; slot=0'
go

and create the key with

create encryption key hsm_key on external keystore
go

create encryption key master
go

create encryption key dbenc_key for database encryption
go

Create a database and encrypt/decrypt etc

At this stage I would like to understand if there were any commands or functions that would safely confirm access to the HSM (ie a command that completes successfully, reports upon availability - or is guaranteed to cause using the HSM)


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Looking for repeatable step/command to confirm connec...
entry.content:
It appears that ASE does not provide a dedicated command to verify HSM connectivity. Instead, you can try creating a master key with the HSM key in a user database or a temp database (one that does not already have a master key). If the master key is created successfully and no related errors appear in the server error log, this confirms that the HSM is available and functioning

For example:

  •  master key can be created, then HSM is available:

1> create encryption key master with external key

go

2> 1> sp_encryption help

2> go

 Key Name                         Key Owner

       Key Length

       Key Algorithm

       Key Type                                 Pad

       Initialization Vector

       Protected By

       Key Recovery

       # of Key Copies

 -------------------------------- ----------------------------------------

       ------------------------------------------

       ------------------------------------------------

       ---------------------------------------- ----------------------------

       ----------------------------------------------------------------

       ----------------------------------------------

       ----------------------------------------------

       ----------------------------------------------------

 sybencrmasterkey                 dbo

                         256

       AES

       symmetric master key                                  0

                                      0

       hsm key

                             0

                                0

 

(1 row affected)

(return status = 0)

 

 

  •  master key can’t be created, then HSM is not available
  • 1> create encryption key master with external key
  • go
  • 2> 1> sp_encryption help
  • 2> go
  • Msg 19603, Level 16, State 1:
  • Procedure 'sp_encryption', Line 2090:
  • There are no encryption keys (key copies) like '%' in 'tempdb'.
  • (return status = 1)
  •  
  • Example server errorlog: 
  • 00:0002:00000:00000:2026/03/23 07:17:21.78 kernel  hsm__encrypt_key_n_salt: Could not found unique key on HSM device with UUID '5074faa41b134e48b58a8b86f97ac800'

entry.title: User not able to share bookmarks in sac stories
entry.content: Users are unable to find other users in the user list when attempting to share a personal bookmark with other SAC users who have same role. The data model resides in Datasphere, where the user has a "consume" role. Furthermore, in SAC, the user has a "standard viewer" role and view/share access to the relevant story.

entry.title: Chamada para Palestrantes - SITREC2026
entry.content:
Chamada para Palestrantes

Se você tem conhecimento para compartilhar, esta é sua oportunidade! Envie sua proposta com o tema, título e uma sinopse para: recife@sitbrazil.com o tempo de palestra é de no máximo 40 minutos. Envie propostas até 10 de Abril de 2026. Aguarde o contato da nossa equipe com a confirmação.

Agenda: A definir

Página do evento:

https://community.sap.com/t5/recife-events/sap-inside-track-recife-2026-edi%C3%A7%C3%A3o-dos-10-anos-do-sitrec/ev-p/14355897

entry.title: Chamada para Patrocinadores
entry.content:
Chamada para Patrocinadores

Apoie o evento da Comunidade SAP. Contato: recife@sitbrazil.com 

Agenda: A definir

Página do evento:

https://community.sap.com/t5/recife-events/sap-inside-track-recife-2026-edi%C3%A7%C3%A3o-dos-10-anos-do-sitrec/ev-p/14355897

entry.title: Usernames created as old-username@domain-n (where n is increment from 1)
entry.content:
I know this is standard procedure for SAP SuccessFactors (https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2835790) - but we see that we have several seasonal workers in different employments during the years and som har domain-4 or -5 as username. This gives us the following headaches: 
1. We sync usernames to external non-SAP payroll. This fails, as username in payroll needs to be a valid email address

2. We do also write username from Entra ID / Azure AD to SuccessFactors, and we are well stocked with errors now, as "username already exists". For Entra ID see the user as the same user, but in SuccessFactors the user may be on his 4th employment - where the 1st employment (now inactive) is user@domain.com, 2nd employent (inactive) is user@domain.com-1, 3rd employment (inactive) is user@domain.com-2 and the active is user@domain.com-3  

Is there a good way to automatically switch usernames so the last active can be without the number-increment? Or do anyone have any other good calls for this?

Thank you !

entry.title: Re: Validation of document item text does not work with a...
entry.content: Hi Chris, Thank you very much for the explanation. It all makes sense to me now. My client’s aim is to ensure that no document line is created without an item text, regardless of the account type. I’m looking for a solution that requires as little effort as possible. With the validation, I’d hoped to avoid having to customize 30 field status groups. I’d also have had less work with the field status of posting keys, but that setting is no longer available in the public cloud, is it? Can’t we set a specific app or app ID as a precondition, and then work with two rules? One rule checks ‘SupplierInvoiceItemText’ in the ‘Create Supplier Invoice’ app, and the other checks DocumentItemText in all other apps??? Best regards, Rado

entry.title: Re: Can you add a notes field in ONB2 to track additional...
entry.content:
Yes, 
You can add a custom object but that might be a overkill, EC field might be enough.

can you please share some examples of the info you want to track?

so you have story reporting? if you want to latter report on the fields you need to consider where you put it.

entry.title: Re: Usernames created as old-username@domain-n (where n i...
entry.content: You will need to find a way of adding a prefix to the username of an inactive employee.

entry.title: Re: Integration Center : is it possible to add columns to...
entry.content:
does adding a new column with a null/empty value helps?
If not you should be doing a EDI/Stacked Fixed Width integration type

entry.title: Tabela de vínculo de número de série em esboço (Draft) de NF de Saída no SAP Business One
entry.content:
Olá pessoal,

Preciso identificar em qual tabela é realizado o vínculo entre item da linha, e número de série em esboço de NF de Saída.

Quando selecionamos o número de série, e adicionamos a NF de Saída como esboço, ao fechar a tela e voltar no esboço, o número de série segue atribuído ao item, o que significa que isso fica registrado em alguma tabela.
Qual seria?

 

 

Encontrei apenas o vínculo para itens controlados por lote, que é por meio da tabela (DRF16 -> OBTN), quando estamos falando de esboço especificamente.
Para números de série não encontro.

Alguém poderia me ajudar? Por favor

Atte,

Gabriel Camargo

SAP Business One

entry.title: Re: Requested Delivery Date copied from Quotation to Sale...
entry.content: There is an open Customer Influence Request to adjust the copy control of the Quote to Sales Order that you can vote for. https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/335739/?section=sectionDetails

entry.title: Re: Publish/Activate an oData V4 Service Group in SAP
entry.content: @S0027140105 You mean trial and error until I found the correct app associated to it? Are there better ways like a table or tcode? Thank you. @Jocelyn_Dart

entry.title: Intercompany customer invoices without reference to SD billing
entry.content:
How to post Intercompany customer invoices without reference to SD billing?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Intercompany customer invoices without reference to S...
entry.content:
Intercompany customer invoices can be posted using the Complex Posting functionality in Create Outgoing Invoices app without reference to SD billing

  1. Access the app Create Outgoing invoices and provide the company code, customer account, Invoice date, Posting Date, Amount and other required fields.
  2. Click on Menu -> Environment -> Complex Posting (F6)
  3. In the Other Items section, provide the posting key, customer account and the intercompany code and press enter
  4. Provide the amount and click on Post
  5. The Cross-company code no will be displayed on the screen

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!

entry.title: Re: Why sustainability is both a responsibility and an opportunity for a Hospital ?
entry.content:
This post was absolutely great!

Thank you so much for the explanation of this healthcare space with Sustainability products!

entry.title: Re: SAP BusinessObjects BI platform taking considerable t...
entry.content:
This is a common phenomenon when installing BO on a virtual machine. If the system runs for a long time without any user interaction, the installation task receives a lower priority. As a result, the process slows down significantly and almost goes into a sleep state.

To “wake up” the installation, simply click on the installation dialog.     Shortly after you do that, you’ll notice new messages appearing, starting with “cleaning …”.

Based on my experience with many installations, the entire installation/update process usually takes between 35 and 50 minutes, depending on the hard drive’s read/write performance.
...
I hope it helps

entry.title: Re: SAP RDP Integration Content for Packaging Fee Replication - Deployment on Edge Integration Cell?

Best regards,

entry.title: Positioning SAP Customer Loyalty Management: Turning Loyalty into a Growth Engine
entry.content:
In today’s hyper-competitive digital landscape, customer loyalty is no longer just about points and rewards—it’s about building meaningful, personalized relationships that drive long-term value. This is where SAP Customer Loyalty Management stands out as a powerful solution within the SAP Customer Experience portfolio.

What is SAP Customer Loyalty Management?

SAP Customer Loyalty Management is a cloud-native, SaaS solution designed to help organizations create, manage, and scale loyalty programs across multiple brands, markets, and channels.

Unlike traditional loyalty systems, it acts as a central hub for customer engagement, enabling businesses to transform loyalty programs from cost centers into revenue-generating strategic assets.

At its core, the solution empowers companies to:

  • Deliver personalized, data-driven experiences
  • Manage loyalty programs globally from a single platform
  • Build stronger emotional connections with customers
  • Turn customers into long-term brand advocates

Why Positioning Matters

Positioning SAP Customer Loyalty Management is not about listing features—it’s about aligning the solution with real business challenges.

Modern businesses face:

  • Fragmented customer data
  • Low engagement and retention
  • Difficulty proving ROI from loyalty initiatives

SAP addresses these challenges by reframing loyalty as a strategic growth driver, not just a marketing tactic by:

  • Centralizing loyalty programs across brands and markets
  • Delivering personalized, omnichannel experiences
  • Providing a unified customer “wallet” for seamless engagement
  • Tracking ROI to prove real business impact

The shift:

Loyalty is no longer a cost center—it’s a revenue-generating engine that boosts retention, engagement, and customer lifetime value.

Bottom line:

With SAP, companies don’t just manage loyalty—they build meaningful customer relationships that drive growth.

Our Course featuring SAP Customer Loyalty Management:

Positioning SAP Customer Loyalty Management

entry.title: Notifications in SAP Cloud ALM are not appearing as available options for some user

entry.title: Re: EWM Outbound delivery - how to update a custom status...
entry.content: Thankyou everyove for the different solution points given in this post. I have also similar issue . But in my case I want to remove DOY status . How can I do that . Because due to different user issues, some times we have to reset DOY status directly in table /SCWM/DB_STATUS in production for a particular FDO and then do some reversals and with normal process it sets back . Can anyone help me . Because with the codes and DOY report it only sets the status but I have to do reverse of it .

entry.title: Introducing Performance Analyzer in SAP HANA Cloud Central
entry.content:
Overview 

SAP HANA Cloud Central now includes the Performance Analyzer, a new capability for visualizing and analyzing historical performance data of SAP HANA Cloud database instances. This feature enables administrators to identify performance issues, investigate root causes, and make data-driven decisions based on up to 42 days of historical metrics. 

This blog post provides an overview of the Performance Analyzer, its key features, and guidance on how to use it effectively. 

Background 

Performance troubleshooting in cloud database environments requires visibility into historical data. While real-time monitoring provides insight into current system state, identifying the performance degradation or correlating issues with specific events requires access to historical metrics. 

The Performance Analyzer addresses this need by providing comprehensive historical data visualization, interactive exploration capabilities, and customizable views for different analysis scenarios. 

Key Features 

Load History Chart 

The Load History chart serves as the primary navigation tool within the Performance Analyzer. It displays up to 42 days of historical data for selected key performance indicators (KPIs), enabling administrators to: 

  • Identify time periods with anomalous behavior 
  • Select specific time ranges for detailed analysis 
  • Correlate performance changes with system events 

The chart includes two sections: an overview panel showing the full 42-day history, and a detail panel that displays granular data for the selected time range. 

Chart Library 

The Performance Analyzer includes a Chart Library containing visualization components organized by category: 

  • CPU, Memory, Lock, Transaction, Workload, Delta merge, Statement, Session, Savepoint 

Administrators can browse charts by category, search for specific visualizations, and preview charts before adding them to the current view. 

Preset and Custom Views 

The Performance Analyzer provides preset views optimized for common analysis scenarios: 

CPU: Charts relevant to processor utilization and statement performance 

Memory: Charts focused on memory consumption patterns 

Lock: Charts for transactional lock and concurrency analysis 

Administrators can also create custom views by modifying preset configurations, adding charts from the library, and saving personalized layouts for future use. 

Interactive Exploration 

All charts within the Performance Analyzer support interactive data exploration: 

  • Tooltips display precise values when hovering over data points 
  • Time range selections synchronize across all charts in the view 
  • Zoom and pan controls enable detailed examination of specific periods 

Accessing the Performance Analyzer 

The Performance Analyzer is accessible through multiple entry points within SAP HANA Cloud Central: 

Side Navigation 

Select the Performance Analyzer icon in the left navigation panel to access the tool directly. 

Instance Overview 

From the instance Overview page, navigate to the Memory or Compute cards and select "Analyze Performance." 

Alerts Integration 

When viewing alerts related to CPU, memory, or transactional locks, select "Analyze Performance Issue" in the alert details to open the Performance Analyzer with the relevant time context. 

Command Palette 

Open the Command Palette, search for "Performance Analyzer," and select the target instance from the results. 

Joule Integration 

The Performance Analyzer is also accessible through Joule. Request performance analysis or enter "Performance Analyzer" to receive navigation assistance. 

Use Cases 

Analyzing CPU Utilization 

To investigate CPU-related performance issues: 

  1. Access the Performance Analyzer and select the CPU preset view 
  2. Use the Load History chart to identify periods of elevated CPU usage 
  3. Select the time range of interest to view detailed metrics 
  4. Add relevant charts such as "Total Execution Time by Statement Hash" 

Analyze which statements or operations contributed to CPU consumption 

Investigating Memory Consumption 

To examine memory usage patterns: 

  1. Select the Memory preset view 
  2. Review the Load History chart for unusual consumption trends 
  3. Add memory allocator charts to identify contributing components 

Examine host allocator visualizations to determine memory distribution 

Troubleshooting Performance Degradation 

To investigate general performance degradation: 

  1. Use the Load History chart to establish baseline performance patterns 
  2. Identify the time when degradation began 
  3. Compare metrics from before and during the performance issue 

Add relevant charts to analyze changes in system behavior 

Summary 

The Performance Analyzer in SAP HANA Cloud Central provides administrators with comprehensive tools for historical performance analysis. By combining extensive data retention, flexible visualization options, and multiple access methods, it enables efficient identification and resolution of performance issues in SAP HANA Cloud environments.

entry.title: 🌍 Comunidade SAP Joinville esta crescendo. Sejam Bem Vindos!
entry.content:
Queremos agradecer a chegada dos novos membros à Comunidade SAP Joinville 🙏

A nossa comunidade é construída por pessoas.
E é isso que faz ela crescer, ganhar relevância e gerar valor real.

Se você chegou agora, aproveite para se apresentar nos comentários:
o que você faz hoje? em qual área do SAP atua ou quer atuar?

👉 Compartilhe suas experiências
👉 Traga aprendizados do dia a dia
👉 Interaja com outros profissionais

Temos recebido pedidos para criação de grupos externos, mas acreditamos que a força da comunidade está aqui.
É aqui que conseguimos conectar pessoas, compartilhar conhecimento e gerar valor para todos.

A comunidade é construída por pessoas.
E é na troca que ela cresce e evolui.

#SAPCommunity #SITJoinville #SAPBrasil #SAPInsideTrack #ComunidadeSAP

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Brasil 2026!!!! Muitos eventos rolando pelo País e você vai ficar de fora?
entry.content:
SAP Inside Track Joinville foi um dia incrível, marcado por conexões, troca de experiências e muito aprendizado.
14/03/2026

entry.title: Re: 🌍 Comunidade SAP Joinville esta crescendo. Sejam Bem Vindos!
entry.content:
Oi, pessoal! Sou a Gislâine 😊
Atuo como gerente de projetos no ecossistema SAP, hoje à frente de PMO e Consultoria.

Sou certificada PMP, Scrum Master e SAP Activate, e também atuo como curadora da Comunidade SAP Joinville.

Meu projeto mais recente foi o SAP Inside Track Joinville, que tenho muito orgulho em fazer parte 💟

Tenho trabalhado com implantação de soluções e gestão de portfólio, e gosto muito de trocar experiências e aprender com a comunidade.

👀Se você tiver curiosidade sobre a trilha de carreira em gestão de projetos, me chama. Será um prazer contribuir!

entry.title: SAP Inside Track São Paulo FUNC 2026
entry.content:
The event will take place on July 26th, starting at 8:00AM UTC and end at 18:00PM UTC.

An SAP Inside Track is a community event, open for all people from the Community (Customers, Consultants and Freelancers). No matter if you are a developer, business consultant or an architect. The event is organized by peers from the community.. and YOU are able to influence the agenda.

More information:

Language: Portuguese and English

Costs: 60 BRL -  Sympla will be open on May 1th 2026

Sponsored By: SAP Community

*Please note that this event entry is a summarized version of the original event and your registration may be required by the organizer shared on the original event website. RSVP accuracy here is without guarantee and does not constitute registration.

Location 

  • @PUC Marques de Paranaguá  - Auditório Marques de Paranaguá, 111

Organization:

Roberta Fernandes

Edson Thomaz

Marssel Vilaça

João Secco

Social Media:

INSTAGRAM SITBR

LINKEDIN SITSP

More information  will be publish in our media, please follow  @sitbrazil

Use #sitsp2026 hashtag, use @_sitsp for mentioning us.

entry.title: Re: Automatically Remove Billing Blocks in Sales Order Billing Plans When Milestones Are Completed
entry.content: Thank you for sharing such a valuable post Aiden! Step by Step posts like this helps everyone understand better how each new functionality works in our Cloud ERP.

entry.title: The AI Revolution in CX: How SAP Business AI is Redefining the Customer Journey
entry.content:
Today’s customers demand intelligent, personalized experiences. To deliver, businesses need more than just good intentions—they need powerful, embedded intelligence. SAP is delivering this with SAP Business AI, a comprehensive strategy that infuses the entire Customer Experience (CX) suite with practical, powerful artificial intelligence, fronted by its conversational copilot, Joule.

This isn't just about AI for your customers; it's about AI for your entire organization.

 AI for Your Frontline Teams: Smarter Engagement

SAP Business AI works out-of-the-box to make your daily operations more intelligent. With Joule as their copilot, your teams can work faster and smarter.

  • For Sales: Joule drafts personalized emails and summarizes opportunities, while embedded AI scores leads so reps can focus on the deals most likely to close.
  • For Service: Joule instantly summarizes complex customer tickets, and AI recommends the best knowledge base solutions, slashing resolution times and boosting satisfaction.
  • For Commerce: AI-powered search and product recommendations create a bespoke shopping experience for every single visitor, driving conversion and loyalty.

AI for Your Project Teams: Faster Implementations

SAP’s AI vision goes deeper, empowering the people who build your CX solutions with Joule for Consultants (J4C).

Think of J4C as an expert "AI librarian" for your project team. It’s a specialized copilot that provides instant, conversational access to SAP's vast knowledge base. Consultants can ask for best practices, configuration guidance, and even code intelligence. By mastering smart prompting, teams can dramatically reduce project complexity and accelerate delivery with confidence.

Ready to Master SAP Business AI?

Dive deep by enrolling in the official SAP Learning course and earning your Badge!

Introducing SAP Business AI for SAP Customer Experience

entry.title: Re: Week 4 : Use Joule Predictive Code Completion to Implement Determination
entry.content:
Week 4 - Completed  (Package: ZAC_2263470)

TASK 1

 

TASK 2

Testing:

Thank you!!

entry.title: Re: ACL is not considered in PS-IS reporting in S/4HANA!
entry.content:
Hello, ACLs have been modernized by provisioning the ability to read ACLs based on CDS views, so, as of SAP S/4HANA 2021, with SAP Note 3641266 then ACLs are no longer part of the compatibility scope. That means you can use it again. But ACL function will not be updated anymore. 

From SAP S/4HANA 2025, SAP recommends transitioning to object-based authorization in project system. You can find more information about the object-based authorization in SAP Note 3646104.

entry.title: Re: Full Picture on Financial Accounting in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content: Hi - it appears as though this Record of Achievement is no longer available. Is this a glitch or was it intentionally removed?

entry.title: Re: Scoping Order in SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS01 – A Detailed Walk-through
entry.content:
Excellent overview of the Maintenance Order Scoping functionality in S/4HANA 2025 FPS01.
The ability to structurally define and control order scope upfront significantly improves planning accuracy, execution clarity, and cost governance.
This is a strong step forward in enabling standardized, fit‑to‑standard maintenance processes with better operational transparency.

entry.title: Re: Procurement of Services in SAP Ariba
entry.content:
@akashbansode-34 

Hello, Thanks for this update. I

I noticed under section 'Other Service Procurement related Topics' (Point 4: Service Entry Sheet Import / Export).
 
However, I have a question:
 
Is the ExportServiceSheet web service intended for data from Ariba Buying and Invoicing solutions into SAP ERP systems?
 
In our current scenario, the SES is created by the supplier on SAP Business Network (SBN).
 
We have activated the relevant parameters, set up the endpoint, and completed all integration configurations.
 
But when monitoring the ICM log, we found that the ExportServiceSheet web service was not triggered at all.
 
I would like to confirm:
 
Is the ExportServiceSheet web service limited to Ariba Buying and Invoicing only?
 
Therefore, when an SES is created in SBN, this web service will not be triggered?
thanks and best Regards,
Berry

entry.title: not able to check input/output parameters in Monitor PPD when runtime environment is PrCo
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Scenario - as we are developing some of the PPD on production process for shop floor execution and testing those PPD (manually or by sending data from shop floor system) But we are unable to check input params sent to the PPD and calculation Output generated by @calulation action block. (attached in below screenshot)

Question - is there any way to check the execution of PPD that has runtime environment selected as Production connector if yes, please let us know how we can check.

 

 

Quick help will be appreciated. 

Regards,
Ankit Gupta

entry.title: EWM practice system
entry.content: Has anybody worked on EWM practice system? I am not  able to set up distribution model from ERP to EWM practice systems. Has anybody faced this problem?

entry.title: CBC behavior when an activated scope item is deprecated or removed
entry.content:
In SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC), I have a question about SAP upgrades where the scope item itself becomes deprecated or planned for removal, not cases where apps or features used within a scope item are deprecated.

If a scope item that is already activated in CBC becomes deprecated or scheduled for removal, and a successor scope item is introduced:

  • Will the successor scope item be automatically activated during the upgrade?
  • Will it be automatically applied or transported to the customer systems (e.g. test / production)?
  • What happens to the original scope item that is deprecated or removed?
    • Is it automatically deactivated or deleted during the upgrade?

Or does this scenario require manual scope adjustment and migration activities by the customer or implementation team?

entry.title: SAP Maintenance Planner options for S/4HANA Upgrade
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,
I'm currently preparing an SAP S/4HANA on-premise upgrade (from 2021 to 2023) and working with Maintenance Planner to generate the stack XML for SUM.
I would like to ask for which options should be selected during Maintenance Planner step Select OS/DB dependent files 

Here is my upgrade plan with Application OS is on Window and Database OS is on Linux ( I already upgrade the SAP HANA DB version and now i want to know the correct option to choose for the future SUM running)

And here are the options in  Maintenance Planner step Select OS/DB dependent files 

-------------

---------------

Thank you.

entry.title: Questiosn regarding onPrem systems
entry.content:
Hello SAP,

It appears that, with our * account, we can only view the * Installation. However, our * contract should also include other environments, such as the * Installation and the  BW Installation. These installations currently appear under our * account, but not under the * one.

Could you please help me understand what actions are required on our side to ensure that the Installation becomes visible in the * account?

I am not requesting the migration at this stage—only the list of steps to anticipate. We need to prepare in advance to avoid any issues, such as creating the necessary users on the* and informing them that they will have to create their cases in the new SAP4me environment, etc.

Best regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Questiosn regarding onPrem systems
entry.content:
According to the KBA 2459283 - How to link customer numbers using the Corporate Group function (CCC) - SAP for Me , adding a subsidiary to a CCC group requires the CCC group's Super admin to open a case. Therefore, please contact the Super admin listed below to open a new case on your behalf. 
After linking * to the CCC group, please also ask the Super admin to assign the appropriate authorizations to the S-users under *.

Relevant authorizations:

  • Report Technical Problem on CCC level
  • Send Cases to SAP on CCC level
  • Display Cases on CCC level
  • Display All Cases on Global Level
  • Close Cases on CCC level
  • Display cloud data（only apply to cloud installations）

entry.title: Question about API-based depreciation expense allocation for fixed assets across multiple company co
entry.content:
Current Situation

We are investigating whether it is possible to execute depreciation expense allocation for fixed assets via API for multiple company codes in parallel.

At the moment, our processing is executed sequentially in the following order:

  • Company A data → API processing completed
  • Company B data → API processing completed

The customer is asking whether the process can be changed so that the data for Company A and Company B can be processed at the same time in parallel, in order to reduce processing time.

Detailed Background

Our understanding is as follows:

  • The target business process is depreciation expense allocation for company fixed assets.
  • The customer would like to improve performance by processing multiple company codes simultaneously.
  • The question is not only about general batch processing, but specifically about whether parallel execution is supported or safe when the same fixed asset may be involved.
  • We would like to understand whether SAP has any official guidance, best practice, limitation, or known behavior for this kind of scenario.

Questions

  1. Is it technically supported to execute depreciation expense allocation for fixed assets via API for multiple company codes in parallel?
  2. If parallel execution is possible, are there any official SAP documents, API guides, best practices, or restrictions that describe this behavior?
  3. If multiple parallel API requests update the same fixed asset at nearly the same time, can this cause:
    • lock conflicts,
    • update conflicts,
    • or failed updates?
  4. If anyone has implemented a similar process, could you please share:
    • the API/service used,
    • whether parallel execution worked,
    • any issues encountered,
    • and how those issues were avoided?

Thank you very much for your support.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration on CF - How to Setup Secure HTTP Inbound Connection with Client Certificates
entry.content:
Hello Mandy,

Can the client certificate be self signed from the sender? Or it should be CA signed, then only will be accepted by BTP load balancer?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Moving Average Price is not getting updated
entry.content: If your goal is for the Purchase Order price to be the source of truth for the material's value at the time of receipt, Price Control V is the only way to achieve this without manual pre-work. Price Control S is designed specifically to ignore the PO price in favor of a pre-calculated standard, which is logically impossible for a batch that is being born during the GRN.

entry.title: Re: Credly certificate
entry.content: hi @Margit Wagner-I also finished https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/discovering-procurement-solutions . Everything is checked off and completed but under My Learning it is stuck at Unit 4 and I still did not get my credly badge for this. Can you help?

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration - Upgrade Readiness Design Guidelines/Best Practices for Future Readiness
entry.content:
Hello, 

Yes, it is possible to upgrade the scripts in the script collection. It was not avaialble in Jan 2026 however with the  Feb 2026 release,  the expected enhancements is available. 

Regards,

Kamlesh.

entry.title: Re: SAP System Changes Required – Transition from Income-...
entry.content:
Wait for the official note by SAP for ECC.

As per the new income tax act 2025, there is a change in the nomenclature of withholding office key. i.e., for e.g., TDS section 194C will be known as 393(1) [Table: S.No. 6(a)] , 393(1) [Table: S.No. 6(b)]  along with new section codes like 1023/1024 representing 194C. These changes in official keys will have implications in TDS/TCS returns. Hence, mapping of old withholding keys with new withholding keys will suffice the requirement in SAP as far as withholding tax changes are concern.

So, instead of creating new keys and associating these new keys with existing tax types & codes, I recommend waiting for note by SAP providing the above mapping, which will suffice your requirement.

entry.title: Re: How to handle two different pay components in a singl...
entry.content: @xavierlegarrec, thank you!

entry.title: JOURNAL ENTRY - FINANCIAL TRANSACTION TYPE FIELD NOT AVAILABLE FOR DERIVATION
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

in order to fulfill the business requirement on differentiating the single position on document entry, we are working on field "Transaction Type" to meet the business needs.

Analyzing the field "Transaction Type" seems not available the standard option to derive the content of the field itself as offered by others SAP releases (i.e. on premise).

Could you please advice about the "Transaction Type" (RMVCT) field? We are expecting the derivation "TPM_DERIVE_ACCNT" being in S/4HANA Public Edition.

Looking forward to yoru kind feedback.

Thanks and Regards,
Massimo


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

The explanation should be enoug to understand/replicate the case in SAP own system.



*Note To SAP:
I give approval for SAP Support to use the Steps to Reproduce I have provided in this workflow, while connected to my impacted non-production environments, even if the steps result in a change being made and my approval remains valid until the issue is resolved, unless I inform SAP Support otherwise.

--- Issue Date (UTC) ---
DIGITS-03-04 23:00:00 - DIGITS-03-17 21:01:55


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: JOURNAL ENTRY - FINANCIAL TRANSACTION TYPE FIELD NOT...
entry.content: The "Financial Transaction Type" (RMVCT) can be derived via app Additional Account Assignments - Substitution Rules.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Deprecation and The Path Forward
entry.content:
It is quite disappointing to learn about this sudden news. We have been continuously researching and refining our use of SAP Build Apps, intending to package it as a unified solution for our customers.

Regarding the innovations that were still visible on the roadmap just last year—such as the deep integration with SAPUI5 and the seamless collaboration between low-code and pro-code—are these features now going to be completely abandoned?

My main question is: moving forward, are low-code development platforms like this going to be entirely replaced by GenAI/GenUI? Or have I misunderstood the future direction?

Best Wishes,

Jack

entry.title: Re: Week 4 : Use Joule Predictive Code Completion to Implement Determination
entry.content:

Package : ZAC_2074454

entry.title: CALLING SAP HANA STORED PROCEDURES WITH VARIABLES
entry.content:
Community

I need to create a stored procedure that will be called from the query manager. I need to include variables so the user can input the values ​​they need, ideally dates and a subtype of an item. If I call the procedure with predefined values, it works, but I need it to be dynamic and allow the user to input that data.

If you could help me with how to structure this or how to call those variables from the query manager ([%0]), I would appreciate it.

entry.title: Outbound IDoc for Hazardous Substances Transfer from ERP to GTS via ALE
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are working on distributing hazardous substances data from ERP to GTS using ALE.

Currently,  we are unable to find the corresponding outbound IDoc in ERP that is responsible for sending hazardous substances data to GTS.

Could anyone please help with:

The outbound IDoc type/message type used for hazardous substances transfer

Relevant configuration steps (ALE/Distribution Model)

Any specific function modules or reports involved in triggering the outbound process

Any pointers or documentation references would be very helpful.

Thanks in advance for your support!

Prasanth Palivela.

entry.title: How to Prepare an Indirect Method Cash Flow Statement in Compliance with Chinese Accounting Standard
entry.content:
I need to prepare a cash flow statement under Chinese Accounting Standards (CAS). There is an issue with the system’s custom analytical queries, so I found the app Cash Flow Statement - Indirect Method. I created a new financial statement version (FSV) to be used for the indirect cash flow statement. However, after selecting this financial statement version and executing the report in the Cash Flow Statement - Indirect Method app, the values in the measure columns are not related to the items in my financial statement version. The measures generated by this app do not match the content of the indirect method under Chinese Accounting Standards.
 
In addition, I have not assigned semantic tags to the financial statement version, because the semantic tags for this app shown in SAP Learning also do not comply with the requirements of Chinese Accounting Standards.
 
(Reference: https://learning.sap.com/courses/financial-statement-reporting-zh/creating-the-cash-flow-statement-indirect-method_bf92692d-7d4d-47d4-93a0-47641afc4485)

entry.title: Re: Usernames created as old-username@domain-n (where n i...
entry.content: May be create an integration job that sets the correct username (without "-n") for the profile is active and set the other inactives with "-n".

entry.title: Re: Updating user on ASC6 table
entry.content: I'm trying to do the same. Have you found a solution?

entry.title: Re: quantity-dependent component scaling - BOM
entry.content: To my knowledge,  for now and the foreseeable future, separate BOMs with production versions remain the standard way to model quantity-dependent scaling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition.

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA CLOUD Workaround 销售含税价
entry.content:
最后一步ZPPR净价格，如果不在VK12 设置物料价格-销售里设置的话，就必须手工在销售订单的定价过程里进行添加，请问有其他方式可以实现自动添加吗？

因为客户这里物料非常多，而且更新频繁，不可能一次性就在“VK12 设置物料价格-销售”里完成所有物料的 ZPPR净价格的初始设置。

entry.title: Query regarding BO 2025 license
entry.content:
We are currently using SAP BO 4.3 SP4 and are planning to migrate to BI DIGITS. Could you please clarify if there will be any additional costs associated with the BI DIGITS license, or will it remain the same as for 4.3 SP4?

Could you advise on the process to obtain the BI DIGITS license key?
 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Query regarding BO 2025 license
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Regarding your issue, kindly know the very first and strong recommendation when it comes to any question about licensing SAP applications and solutions –not to mention pricing– is always to engage in a discussion with your SAP account executive. This will always be the shortest path to the most meaningful answer based on your unique and specific customer context.

So could you pls kindly contact your SAP Account Manager per KBA 2626344 and see if there is any additional cost for BOBJ 2025?

As of BI 2025 license key request, could you pls kindly refer to the detailed steps documented in KBA 2251998 and try to request the new license key for version 2025 on your side?

Should you encounter any technical issue during the license key request, feel free to let us know by creating a case using component XX-SER-LIKEY-BOJ.

Hope the above information helps.

Thanks and regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: CPI SOAP error - HTTP response '400: Bad Request'
entry.content:
Hi,

When an external SOAP service is called via SOAP UI with proper request payload, we get expected response, but when we use the same request payload to call the same Service via SAP CPI, I get the below error

org.apache.cxf.interceptor.Fault: Could not send Message., cause: org.apache.cxf.transport.http.HTTPException: HTTP response '400: Bad Request. Bad Request' when communicating with URL

It was working fine until 06/03/2026. The SOAP vendor doesn't update anything, so I suspect SAP has changed something.

Has anyone got this error before?

Regards,

Tri

entry.title: Re: AI-Augmented Oil Price Scenario Modelling in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
hello @dirkpelz  thanks for sharing the wonderful blog.

Question: Did you face CORS issue while calling an HTTP endpoint from SAC Custom Widget?
My scenario: SAC User story(custom widget) >> call SAP AI core service (or even SAP BTP Cloud foundry app API) >> results must be shown back into SAC User story.

Error: CORS issue while SAC calling  SAP AI core service as below. Pls provide some guidance

Note: Routing the requests via SAP APIM will be my last preferred option as I don't want to introduce additional layer in between.

entry.title: Re: Receiving History
entry.content: You can check in MB03 or via table EKBE

entry.title: Re: Automatic G/L Account Determination Based on Material...
entry.content:
Hello @easap ,


There is no standard configuration to determine G/L directly from Material Type
A)Use valuation class mapping (standard solution)
B)If strict requirement → use BAdI MB_ACCOUNTING_BADI


A)Recommended Solution (Best Practice)


-Define valuation classes per material type
-Assign valuation class in material master
-Maintain OBYC accordingly
This achieves indirect Material Type → G/L mapping

B)Enhancement Approach (Only if unavoidable)

BAdI Implementation
BAdI: MB_ACCOUNTING_BADI

Logic flow:
Read Material Type (MARA-MTART)
Map to desired G/L account
Override account before posting

Summary:-

✔ SAP best practice = Material Type → Valuation Class → G/L Account
❌ Avoid direct Material Type → G/L unless absolutely required

 

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Change number not capturing the routing X-steps changes
entry.content:
Hi Expects,

In a routing updated the x-steps with a change number and saved. While releasing the change number, system saying no changes made with this change number. Kindly explain. Thankyou.

entry.title: Re: Week 3 : Use RAP Business Logic Prediction to Implement Validation
entry.content:
Week 3 Challenge has been completed.

Package: ZAC_1555968

entry.title: Re: fg order start & end date consider production start a...
entry.content: In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, the system meets your requirement through multi-level BOM scheduling combined with routing and lead-time offset logic. The standard approach is to use production versions, routings, and MRP scheduling so that the start/end dates of finished goods are automatically calculated based on the dependent requirements of their multi-level components. Direct “component-level date inheritance” is not configurable at the BOM level, but the system achieves this via MRP and order scheduling.

entry.title: Step-by-step implementation guide for SAP S/4HANA Event Mesh (S4 as Producer, CPI as Consumer)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I’m currently exploring the implementation of SAP Event Mesh (not AEM) with S/4HANA as the event producer and SAP Integration Suite (CPI) as the consumer.

While there are several SAP blogs and documentation references explaining Event Mesh concepts and architecture, I have not been able to find a single end-to-end guide that provides clear step-by-step instructions for implementing the following scenario:

Scenario:
S/4HANA → Event Mesh → SAP CPI (Integration Suite)

Specifically, I am looking for guidance on:

  1. Configuration steps in S/4HANA to enable event publishing (Event Enablement / Enterprise Eventing).

  2. Setting up SAP Event Mesh (queues, topics, service keys, etc.).

  3. Connecting SAP CPI as an event consumer (AMQP / webhook / queue subscription).

  4. Best practices for event topic design and queue subscriptions.

  5. Any recommended patterns for downstream integration (e.g., CPI → S/4 or CPI → third-party systems).

If anyone has implemented this pattern or can share:

  • a step-by-step guide

  • architecture recommendations

  • SAP documentation or reference implementations

it would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: How to do mass G/L accounts' text addition
entry.content: Hi Henrike, thank you for your answer. I have tried using G/L account renumbering as you recommended. However, the editable descriptions are the texts themselves, not the language key. Neither could we add new record with target language key "JA". As a result, the problem has not been solved.

entry.title: Re: Schedule a Query with Default Parameters And Export to Excel
entry.content: Hi Gonzalo, Do you know an add on that can schedule export a query?

entry.title: Re: How to do mass G/L accounts' text addition
entry.content: The problem is exact the same to the following post https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-q-a/massive-add-of-language-key-in-gl-account-sap-public-cloud/qaq-p/14023724

entry.title: Re: Attaching Documents in CALM
entry.content:
It’s now possible if you has access to SAP BTP Document Management Service, you can upload and manage external files.

This feature is available on the details page of a document, under External File.

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/external-files?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: F110 - how to choose one specific IBAN from one vendor
entry.content: In Public Cloud, you cannot manually choose the IBAN in F110. The correct IBAN must be controlled via vendor master data (BP) and payment method/ranking configuration. If you need scenario-based IBAN selection, you’ll need to model that with separate payment methods

entry.title: Re: Extend Your S-User Expiry Date (and Avoid Last-Minute Surprises)
entry.content: Very informative blog. A good reference document for S-User ID extension related queries.

entry.title: Re: CAPEX BUDGET PR/PO LEVEL PUBLIC CLOUD
entry.content:
Below is the standard approaches in Public Cloud

1) Use WBS Elements for CAPEX
Create a CAPEX project with WBS elements. Assign budgets at WBS level. Link PR/PO to the WBS (account assignment P). Budget check happens automatically during PR/PO creation and posting.

2) Settlement to Asset Under Construction (AUC)
Costs collected on the WBS are later settled to an AUC asset. Once the project is complete, settlement moves values from AUC to the final fixed asset. This way, you control CAPEX spending via WBS budgets, while still capitalizing to assets.

3) Budget Reporting
Use apps like Monitor Projects or Project Control – Financials to track budget vs. actuals. The system shows consumption at WBS level, not asset class level.

entry.title: Re: CAPEX BUDGET PR/PO LEVEL PUBLIC CLOUD
entry.content: You tagged as "Private Cloud" but your post says "Public Cloud" - Please maintain the right tag
